summary: A lightweight, Promise-based HTTP client runtime library for browser environments that serves as the RPC transport layer for Conjure-generated TypeScript service clients. Published as the 'conjure-client' npm package, it wraps the Fetch API with Bearer token authentication, structured error handling (ConjureError with typed error categories), configurable retry with exponential backoff, user-agent tracking via a custom Fetch-User-Agent header, and content-type-aware request/response serialization supporting JSON, binary streams, multipart form data, URL-encoded forms, and plain text. The library defines the IHttpApiBridge interface that all Conjure-generated service classes depend on, making it the universal adapter between generated client code and actual HTTP transport.

apiSurface.name: IHttpApiBridge
apiSurface.signature: interface IHttpApiBridge { callEndpoint<T>(parameters: IHttpEndpointOptions): Promise<T>; call<T>(serviceName, endpointName, method, endpointPath, data?, headers?, queryArguments?, pathArguments?, requestMediaType?, responseMediaType?): Promise<T>; }
apiSurface.description: Core bridge interface that all Conjure-generated TypeScript service classes depend on. Defines two methods: callEndpoint (takes an options object) and call (takes positional parameters to reduce code size in generated clients). Every generated service constructor accepts an IHttpApiBridge and delegates all HTTP calls through it.

apiSurface.name: IHttpEndpointOptions
apiSurface.signature: interface IHttpEndpointOptions { serviceName?: string; endpointPath: string; endpointName?: string; headers?: Record<string, string|number|boolean|undefined|null>; method: string; requestMediaType?: MediaType; responseMediaType?: MediaType; pathArguments: any[]; queryArguments: any; data?: any; binaryAsStream?: boolean; }
apiSurface.description: Configuration object for a single HTTP endpoint call. Contains the endpoint path template with {param} placeholders, HTTP method, path/query arguments for interpolation, request/response media types, optional headers, and body data. The serviceName and endpointName fields are metadata for logging only and do not affect the network request.

apiSurface.name: MediaType
apiSurface.signature: enum MediaType { APPLICATION_JSON = 'application/json', APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM = 'application/octet-stream', APPLICATION_X_WWW_FORM_URLENCODED = 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded', MULTIPART_FORM_DATA = 'multipart/form-data', TEXT_PLAIN = 'text/plain' }
apiSurface.description: Enumeration of supported MIME types for request and response content negotiation. JSON is the default for both request and response when not specified. Octet-stream triggers binary handling (ReadableStream or Blob). Multipart form data omits the Content-Type header so the browser sets the form boundary automatically.

apiSurface.name: FetchBridge
apiSurface.description: The primary implementation of IHttpApiBridge, also exported as DefaultHttpApiBridge. Wraps the Fetch API to make HTTP requests with Bearer token auth, user-agent tracking, content-type-aware serialization/deserialization, and binary stream support. Constructor accepts baseUrl (string or supplier), userAgent (single or array of IUserAgent/suppliers), optional token (string, sync supplier, or async supplier), and optional custom fetch function.

apiSurface.name: IFetchBridgeParams
apiSurface.signature: interface IFetchBridgeParams { baseUrl: string | Supplier<string>; userAgent: IUserAgent | ReadonlyArray<IUserAgent | Supplier<IUserAgent | IUserAgent[]>>; token?: string | Supplier<string> | Supplier<Promise<string>>; fetch?: FetchFunction; }
apiSurface.description: Configuration for FetchBridge construction. baseUrl and token support both static values and supplier functions for dynamic resolution. userAgent supports single agents, arrays, and supplier functions that return agents or arrays of agents. An optional custom fetch function can replace the global fetch for testing or Node.js usage.

apiSurface.name: ConjureError
apiSurface.signature: class ConjureError<E> { readonly type: ConjureErrorType; readonly originalError?: any; readonly status?: number; readonly body?: string | E; }
apiSurface.description: Typed error class wrapping all HTTP failures with categorized error types. Contains the error category (Network, Parse, Status, Other), the original error object, HTTP status code (when available), and the response body (when available). Provides toString() for structured JSON serialization of error details.

apiSurface.name: ConjureErrorType
apiSurface.signature: enum ConjureErrorType { Network = 'NETWORK', Other = 'OTHER', Parse = 'PARSE', Status = 'STATUS' }
apiSurface.description: Categorizes ConjureError instances: NETWORK for fetch TypeError (DNS, connectivity), PARSE for JSON parsing failures on response bodies, STATUS for non-ok HTTP responses (4xx/5xx), and OTHER for uncategorized errors. This classification enables callers to handle different failure modes distinctly.

apiSurface.name: isConjureError
apiSurface.signature: function isConjureError(error: unknown): error is ConjureError<unknown>
apiSurface.description: Type guard for ConjureError that works across different module instances by checking both instanceof and constructor name on the prototype chain. Handles null/undefined safely.

apiSurface.name: RetryingFetch
apiSurface.signature: class RetryingFetch { constructor(delegate: FetchFunction, backoffStrategy: BackoffStrategy); get fetch(): FetchFunction; }
apiSurface.description: Decorates a FetchFunction with retry logic. Retries only on HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) and 503 (Service Unavailable) responses. Honors the Retry-After response header (max 60 seconds) when present, otherwise falls back to the configured BackoffStrategy. Does not retry on other error status codes or network errors.

apiSurface.name: exponentialBackoff
apiSurface.signature: function exponentialBackoff(maxNumRetries: number, backoffSlotSizeMs: number): BackoffStrategy
apiSurface.description: Creates an exponential backoff with full jitter strategy. For the c-th retry, randomly selects a delay from [0, backoffSlotSizeMs * 2^c). Returns undefined when maxNumRetries is reached to signal stop.

apiSurface.name: BackoffStrategy
apiSurface.description: Function type for retry backoff strategies. Receives the zero-based attempt number and returns milliseconds to wait, or undefined to stop retrying.

apiSurface.name: IConjureResult
apiSurface.description: Discriminated union result type for Conjure operations. IConjureSuccess has status 'success' with a result field. IConjureFailure has status 'failure' with a ConjureError field. Enables explicit success/failure handling without try/catch.

apiSurface.name: IUserAgent
apiSurface.signature: interface IUserAgent { productName: string; productVersion: string; comments?: string[]; }
apiSurface.description: User agent descriptor following RFC 7231 format. Serialized as 'productName/productVersion (comment1; comment2)' and sent via the custom Fetch-User-Agent header. The conjure-typescript-runtime library automatically appends its own user agent to every request.

apiSurface.name: FetchFunction
apiSurface.signature: type FetchFunction = (url: string | Request, init?: RequestInit) => Promise<IFetchResponse>
apiSurface.description: Type alias for a fetch-compatible function. Allows injecting custom fetch implementations (e.g., node-fetch with stream adapters, or RetryingFetch) into FetchBridge.

apiSurface.name: IFetchResponse
apiSurface.signature: interface IFetchResponse extends IFetchBody { readonly body: ReadableStream<Uint8Array> | null; readonly headers: Headers; readonly ok: boolean; readonly status: number; }
apiSurface.description: Minimal abstraction of the Fetch API Response interface. Extends IFetchBody (json(), text(), blob() methods) with status, ok flag, headers, and optional ReadableStream body. Allows substitution of non-standard fetch implementations.

apiSurface.name: blobToReadableStream
apiSurface.description: Adapter that converts a Blob response to a ReadableStream for environments that do not support response.body. Uses native blob.stream() when MODERN env is set, otherwise uses a FileReader-based polyfill or the web-streams-polyfill ponyfill.

architecturePatterns.description: The IHttpApiBridge interface decouples generated Conjure service clients from HTTP transport details. Generated service classes accept an IHttpApiBridge in their constructor and delegate all network calls through it. This allows different transport implementations (FetchBridge, RetryingFetch-wrapped bridges, custom bridges) to be swapped without changing generated code.

RetryingFetch wraps any FetchFunction with retry-on-429/503 behavior, implementing the same FetchFunction interface. It can be composed with FetchBridge by passing retryingFetch.fetch as the custom fetch parameter, creating a transparent retry layer.

Configuration values (baseUrl, token, userAgent) accept both static values and zero-argument supplier functions. The token supplier can even return a Promise for async token resolution. This enables dynamic token refresh, multi-tenant base URL routing, and per-request user agent updates without recreating the bridge.

Authentication is handled via Bearer token in the Authorization header. The token is resolved at request time (supporting sync/async suppliers for token refresh). If no token is provided, the Authorization header is omitted entirely. Credentials mode is set to 'same-origin' for cookie-based session fallback.

Request body serialization and response deserialization are dispatched based on MediaType. JSON bodies use JSON.stringify/json(), binary uses passthrough/blob(), form-encoded uses query string encoding, multipart omits Content-Type for browser boundary injection. Response parsing checks Content-Type header to choose json(), blob(), or text() deserialization.

Endpoint paths use {param} placeholders that are sequentially replaced with URL-encoded path arguments. Query parameters are separately appended as an encoded query string. This matches the Conjure wire specification for parameterized endpoint paths.

All errors are wrapped in ConjureError with a ConjureErrorType discriminant. TypeError from fetch becomes NETWORK, JSON parse failures become PARSE, non-ok HTTP responses become STATUS (preserving status code and response body), and all others become OTHER. The isConjureError type guard works across module boundaries.

Retry delays use the 'exponential backoff with full jitter' algorithm: for attempt c, delay is randomly chosen from [0, slotSize * 2^c). This reduces thundering herd effects. The Retry-After header takes precedence when present (capped at 60 seconds). Only HTTP 429 and 503 responses trigger retries.

Instead of overriding the standard User-Agent header (restricted by browsers), the library sends a custom 'Fetch-User-Agent' header formatted per RFC 7231 (productName/productVersion with optional comments). The conjure-typescript-runtime version is always appended. This enables server-side request attribution in Palantir service logs.

Binary responses (octet-stream, images, PDFs, Office docs, etc.) are returned as ReadableStream<Uint8Array> when binaryAsStream is true. If response.body is null (older browsers), falls back to a Blob-to-ReadableStream adapter using FileReader or the web-streams-polyfill ponyfill. Modern builds use native blob.stream().

architecturePatterns.pattern: Compact Call API for Code Size Reduction
architecturePatterns.description: The IHttpApiBridge.call() method accepts positional parameters instead of an options object, reducing the total code size of generated clients by eliminating repeated field name strings. Generated code can choose either callEndpoint (readable) or call (compact) based on optimization preferences.

fields.name: serviceName
fields.description: Conjure service name for logging; does not affect the HTTP request

fields.name: endpointPath
fields.description: Path template with {param} placeholders, e.g. '/foo/{param1}/bar'

fields.name: endpointName
fields.description: Conjure endpoint name for logging; does not affect the HTTP request

fields.name: headers
fields.type: Record<string, string | number | boolean | undefined | null> | undefined
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers; null/undefined values are filtered out, others are stringified

fields.name: method
fields.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)

fields.name: requestMediaType
fields.description: MIME type of outgoing request body; defaults to application/json

fields.name: responseMediaType
fields.description: Expected MIME type of server response; defaults to application/json; sets Accept header

fields.name: pathArguments
fields.description: Values sequentially interpolated into {param} placeholders in endpointPath, URL-encoded

fields.name: queryArguments
fields.description: Key-value pairs appended as URL query string; arrays produce repeated keys; null values omitted

fields.name: data
fields.description: Request body data; serialization depends on requestMediaType

fields.name: binaryAsStream
fields.description: When true, binary responses return ReadableStream<Uint8Array> instead of Blob

fields.name: type
fields.description: Error category: NETWORK, PARSE, STATUS, or OTHER

fields.name: originalError
fields.description: The original caught error, if any

fields.name: status
fields.description: HTTP status code, present for STATUS and PARSE errors

fields.name: body
fields.description: Response body for STATUS errors; typed parameter E allows callers to specify expected error body shape

fields.name: status
fields.description: Discriminant literal for success case

fields.name: result
fields.description: The successful operation result

fields.name: status
fields.description: Discriminant literal for failure case

fields.name: error
fields.description: The typed Conjure error

fields.name: productName
fields.description: Product name component of the user agent string

fields.name: productVersion
fields.description: Product version component

fields.name: comments
fields.description: RFC 7231 comment metadata; allowed characters: a-zA-Z0-9.-:_/ and space; joined with semicolons in parentheses

fields.name: baseUrl
fields.description: Base URL for all requests; supports dynamic resolution via supplier function

fields.name: userAgent
fields.description: User agent(s) sent via Fetch-User-Agent header; supports static, array, and supplier patterns

fields.name: token
fields.description: Bearer token for Authorization header; supports static, sync supplier, and async supplier for token refresh

fields.name: fetch
fields.description: Optional custom fetch implementation; defaults to global fetch if not provided

fields.name: body
fields.description: Response body as a readable stream; null in older browsers

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Response headers

fields.name: ok
fields.description: True if status is in 200-299 range

fields.name: status
fields.description: HTTP status code

fields.name: APPLICATION_JSON
fields.description: JSON content type; default for both request and response

fields.name: APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM
fields.description: Binary content type; triggers stream/blob handling

fields.name: APPLICATION_X_WWW_FORM_URLENCODED
fields.description: URL-encoded form data

fields.name: MULTIPART_FORM_DATA
fields.description: Multipart form data; Content-Type header omitted to let browser set boundary

fields.name: TEXT_PLAIN
fields.description: Plain text; objects are rejected

fields.name: Network
fields.description: fetch TypeError indicating DNS/connectivity/CORS failure

fields.name: Other
fields.description: Uncategorized errors

fields.name: Parse
fields.description: Response body parsing failure (e.g. invalid JSON)

fields.name: Status
fields.description: Non-ok HTTP status (4xx/5xx); body and status code preserved
